rationale: Ground truth has zero substantive concerns (bot notice + plain 'lgtm'), so both candidates trivially achieve full recall and both correctly conclude the PR is sound. X gives a clean, accurate, well-verified approve but offers little beyond confirmation. Y does the same hunk-by-hunk verification plus surfaces three grounded, file/line-specific nits (naming-convention divergence, missing chat-completion test coverage) that are genuinely useful and actionable, though one nit slightly mischaracterizes the fix's core purpose as 'incidental' rather than the PR's main intent, costing a bit of precision.
